attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who relies on surviving climbs in reduced groups rather than explosive attacks. Thrives in slow-speed sprint finishes from small groups after sustained climbing efforts.
team_tactics: Heavy reliance on dedicated domestique support for climb pacing (Camna in 2020). Team sets tempo to control selection rather than launching aggressive moves, positioning Martinez for finale opportunism.
terrain_preference: Medium-length climbs that create attrition without requiring explosive accelerations. Best suited to breakaway scenarios where sustained climbing reduces group size for his sprint finish.
vulnerability: Struggles on final rises when expected to perform as GC contender rather than stage hunter. Exposed when forced into defensive racing or when climbs require repeated accelerations over pure endurance.
key_patterns: Succeeds from breakaways with domestique pacing support, not solo aggressive moves (2020 Tour evidence)
Performs better as opportunist stage hunter than designated GC contender role (2020 vs 2022)
Tactical maturity in small-group finales; can win slow-speed sprints after hard climbing (2020 Tour)
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 showed inability to meet GC expectations after 2020 stage success, suggesting role confusion or declining climbing form
